{"parentUuid":"d1132671-6c37-4670-992a-293e7113cc74","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"2cec357f-e1e0-45e7-b68c-d7320c59fa38","agentId":"ad40e130c6ec87fe3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0118kqmkw8hBw9RqigRCKmkw","type":"tool_result","content":"1	**LoRA Fine-Tuning Strategy**
2	
3	**for Rune's Qwen3.5-9B Adapter Pipeline**
4	
5	Elixir Trials --- March 31, 2026 (Rev. 2)
6	
7	1\. Pipeline Overview
8	
9	Rune's adapter pipeline is a single sequential process with two stages.
10	Stage 1 (QLoRA bootstrapping) produces a corpus of high-quality adapters
11	via gradient descent. Stage 2 (hypernetwork training) trains a model to
12	approximate Stage 1's output in real time. The hypernetwork is the
13	production system; QLoRA is the mechanism that creates its training
14	data.
15	
16	The core objective is to produce adapters that encode *procedural
17	knowledge* from coding trajectories---not just factual recall. An
18	adapter generated from a trajectory of "generate → execute → error →
19	diagnose → fix" should enable the model to apply that repair pattern to
20	novel, structurally similar errors. This is distinct from RAG (which
21	consumes context tokens) and from full fine-tuning (which risks
22	catastrophic forgetting).
23	
24	  --------------------- ------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------
25	                        **Stage 1: QLoRA Bootstrapping**                        **Stage 2: Hypernetwork Production**
26	  Purpose               Build high-quality adapter corpus                       Generate adapters in real time during agent loop
27	  Mechanism             Gradient descent on trajectory SFT data                 Single forward pass through DocToLoraHypernetwork
28	  Starting checkpoint   DeltaCoder adapter (warm-start)                         Sakana qwen\_4b\_d2l aggregator (transfer)
29	  Rank                  64 (inherited from DeltaCoder)                          8--32 (see Section 3.3)
30	  Latency               Minutes per adapter (GPU-bound)                         Milliseconds per adapter (CPU-capable)
31	  Quality               High (gradient-optimized)                               Approximate (amortized)
32	  Volume                50--100 adapters (bootstrapping)                        Unlimited (on-demand generation)
33	  When it runs          Offline corpus building + occasional high-value tasks   Real-time within the agent loop
34	  --------------------- ------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------
35	
36	Once the hypernetwork is trained, QLoRA's ongoing role shrinks to
37	producing occasional adapters for tasks where the hypernetwork's
38	approximation is insufficient. Those adapters feed back into periodic
39	hypernetwork retraining.
40	
41	2\. Stage 1: QLoRA Fine-Tuning with DeltaCoder Warm-Start
42	
43	2.1 The DeltaCoder Warm-Start
44	
45	Rather than initializing new LoRA matrices from scratch, Stage 1 loads
46	**danielcherubini/Qwen3.5-DeltaCoder-9B** via
47	PeftModel.from\_pretrained, sets requires\_grad=True on the adapter
48	parameters, and continues training on Rune's trajectory data. This
49	provides three advantages:
50	
51	-   **GDN coverage inherited:** DeltaCoder already targets all 12 module
52	    types (5 GDN + 4 attention + 3 MLP). No manual module list
53	    configuration needed.
54	
55	-   **Convergence acceleration:** Starting from pretrained weights skips
56	    the initial convergence phase. DeltaCoder was trained on 50K
57	    CoderForge tool-call examples and DPO-aligned on self-correction
58	    pairs---directly relevant to Rune's trajectory domain.
59	
60	-   **DPO alignment preserved:** DeltaCoder's DPO stage on AceCode-V2
61	    preference pairs gives it self-correction behavior that maps to
62	    Rune's Phase 5 diagnose→repair loop.
63	
64	Prerequisite: Validate DeltaCoder before warm-starting. Load via
65	PeftModel.from\_pretrained, confirm no shape errors, run ≥50 HumanEval
66	samples. If it degrades the base model, fall back to fresh LoRA
67	initialization using DeltaCoder's target module list as the
68	configuration template.
69	
70	2.2 Training Hyperparameters
71	
72	  ------------------ ---------------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
73	  **Parameter**      **Value**                                      **Rationale**
74	  LoRA rank          64 (inherited from DeltaCoder)                 Matching rank avoids weight\_head reshape; sufficient for code trajectories
75	  LoRA alpha         32 (alpha = rank/2)                            DeltaCoder convention; PEFT scale = 0.5 (conservative)
76	  Learning rate      2e-4                                           Constant schedule with warmup; cosine decay risks premature convergence on small datasets
77	  LR schedule        Constant + warmup (10 steps)                   Avoids oscillation on small trajectory datasets
78	  Loss computation   Assistant tokens only                          Mask system/user turns; compute loss only on model outputs
79	  Attention impl     eager (NOT flash\_attention\_2)                Flash + sample packing causes loss collapse on Qwen3.5
80	  Sequence length    4,096--65,536 tokens                           Depends on trajectory length distribution
81	  Precision          bf16                                           NF4 quantized base, bf16 adapter weights
82	  Batch size         16--32 effective (via gradient accumulation)   Per-device batch=1, grad\_accum=16--32
83	  Per-turn EOS       Yes                                            Train clean stop at turn boundaries
84	  Quantization       NF4 double-quantized                           Matches existing trainer.py; VRAM \~12--17GB
85	  Epochs             3                                              Standard for small trajectory datasets
86	  ------------------ ---------------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
87	
88	2.3 GDN-Aware Target Modules
89	
90	The full target module list for Qwen3.5-9B, inherited from DeltaCoder:
91	
92	  ----------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------
93	  **Category**            **Modules**                                                       **Layers Affected**
94	  GDN projections         in\_proj\_qkv, in\_proj\_z, in\_proj\_b, in\_proj\_a, out\_proj   24/32 (indices where i%4 != 3)
95	  Attention projections   q\_proj, k\_proj, v\_proj, o\_proj                                8/32 (indices 3,7,11,\...,31)
96	  MLP projections         gate\_proj, up\_proj, down\_proj                                  All 32 layers
97	  ----------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------
98	
99	When warm-starting from DeltaCoder, this module list is already baked
100	into the adapter's config. When initializing fresh (fallback case), the
101	list must be set explicitly.
102	
103	2.4 Training Data Pipeline
104	
105	1.  **Collection:** The rune-agent loop (generate → execute → reflect)
106	    produces CodingSession trajectories stored as JSON via trajectory.py
107	
108	2.  **Formatting:** format\_for\_sft() converts trajectories to
109	    3-message chat format (system, user, assistant).
110	    format\_for\_distillation() handles the KL-divergence variant
111	
112	3.  **Augmentation:** augment\_trajectories() generates LLM-augmented
113	    variants via Ollama (3 variants per trajectory by default)
114	
115	4.  **Splitting:** split\_by\_task\_id() ensures train/test splits
116	    preserve task\_id integrity (no data leakage)
117	
118	Both successful and failed trajectories are training signals. Successful
119	trajectories encode working solution patterns. Exhausted trajectories
120	(max attempts reached) encode diagnose→repair patterns critical for
121	error recovery. The initial corpus should span at least 3 task types
122	(bug-fix, feature-impl, refactor).
123	
124	3\. Stage 2: Hypernetwork Training
125	
126	Once Stage 1 produces the adapter corpus, the hypernetwork is trained to
127	approximate QLoRA's output in a single forward pass. The hypernetwork is
128	the component that makes Rune's real-time adapter generation
129	possible---without it, every new trajectory would require minutes of
130	GPU-bound gradient descent before it could inform the model's behavior.
131	
132	3.1 Sakana Aggregator Transfer
133	
134	Training the hypernetwork from random initialization has been shown to
135	fail (farpluto/doc-to-lora-niah-qwen3.5-2B achieves 0% exact-match on
136	NIAH). The recommended path:
137	
138	1.  **Download** qwen\_4b\_d2l **checkpoint** from Sakana's
139	    SakanaAI/doc-to-lora repository. This variant is preferred over
140	    gemma\_demo or mistral\_7b\_d2l because its aggregator was trained
141	    on a Qwen-family model, making feature representations more
142	    transferable.
143	
144	2.  **Transfer aggregator weights** via transfer\_aggregator\_weights():
145	    loads aggregator.\* parameters, freezes them (requires\_grad=False).
146	
147	3.  **Retrain head.\* only** against the Stage 1 adapter corpus. This
148	    leverages Sakana's pretrained Perceiver feature representations
149	    while adapting the output projection to Qwen3.5-9B's architecture
150	    and Rune's trajectory domain.
151	
152	Note the distinction: DeltaCoder is the warm-start for *Stage 1* (the
153	QLoRA adapters). Sakana is the warm-start for *Stage 2* (the
154	hypernetwork itself). They operate on different components of the
155	system.
156	
157	3.2 KL-Divergence Context Distillation
158	
159	The training loop uses a two-pass strategy per step: (1) extract
160	activations from the base model given the trajectory in-context
161	(teacher), (2) generate adapter weights via the hypernetwork and apply
162	them to the base model with no trajectory in context (student), (3)
163	compute the blended loss:
164	
165	L = α · KL(student \|\| teacher) + (1 - α) · CE(student, labels)
166	
167	where α=0.5. The adapter must reconstruct the teacher's behavior from
168	weight-space alone---this is what makes it a *parametric* episodic
169	memory rather than a context-based one. An enhancement worth testing:
170	use base+DeltaCoder as the teacher instead of base alone, giving the
171	hypernetwork a stronger target distribution to approximate.
172	
173	3.3 Hypernetwork Rank: Why Rank-8, and When to Increase
174	
175	The current default rank of 8 comes from the Sakana qwen\_4b\_d2l
176	checkpoint, which was trained at that rank. Starting at rank-8 preserves
177	the transfer benefit. But rank-8 may be too conservative for
178	Qwen3.5-9B's 12-module GDN architecture, and the rank is the primary
179	knob controlling the expressiveness vs. cost tradeoff of
180	hypernetwork-generated adapters.
181	
182	The key constraint is the weight\_head output dimension, which scales
183	linearly with rank:
184	
185	  ----------------------- ----------------------------------- ----------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
186	  **Hypernetwork Rank**   **weight\_head Output (approx.)**   **weight\_head Parameters**   **Practical Concern**
187	  8                       \~25M                               \~200M                        Fits current architecture; Sakana transfer applies directly
188	  16                      \~50M                               \~400M                        2× cost; likely the sweet spot for expressiveness vs. size
189	  32                      \~100M                              \~800M                        Approaching diminishing returns; consider chunked heads
190	  64                      \~200M                              \~1.6B                        weight\_head larger than some target models; not recommended for single linear projection
191	  ----------------------- ----------------------------------- ----------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
192	
193	The Perceiver latent is 32 × 256 = 8,192 dimensions. Projecting from
194	8,192 to 200M (rank-64) via a single linear layer is a 1.6B-parameter
195	matrix---expensive to train and prone to overfitting on 50--100
196	trajectories. At rank-16 or above, the single weight\_head should be
197	replaced with a per-module or chunked head architecture. The
198	per\_rank\_gen flag in d2l\_config.py (currently set to False) provides
199	a starting point: it generates each rank dimension independently, which
200	distributes the projection across smaller matrices.
201	
202	**Recommendation:** Start at rank-8 to validate the pipeline end-to-end
203	with Sakana transfer. Once working, sweep rank ∈ {8, 16, 32} and measure
204	Pass\@1 vs. generation latency. If rank-16 closes \>80% of the gap to
205	QLoRA rank-64, adopt it. Enable per\_rank\_gen when rank exceeds 16.
206	
207	3.4 Training Parameters
208	
209	  ------------------- ------------------------------ ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
210	  **Parameter**       **Value**                      **Source**
211	  Latent dimensions   32 latents × 256 dims          DocToLoraHypernetwork defaults
212	  Perceiver depth     4 layers, 8 heads              DocToLoraHypernetwork defaults
213	  Output rank         8 (initial; ablation target)   Sakana checkpoint + sweep plan
214	  Learning rate       2e-4                           D2LTrainConfig default
215	  KL blend α          0.5                            Equal weight to KL divergence and CE loss
216	  Temperature         2.0                            KL distillation temperature
217	  Gradient clipping   1.0                            D2LTrainConfig default
218	  Warmup steps        10                             D2LTrainConfig default
219	  Max input length    512 tokens                     Activation extraction window
220	  Scaling factor      0.16                           Bayesian-optimized (200 trials); Sakana default of 45.25× is far too aggressive
221	  ------------------- ------------------------------ ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
222	
223	4\. Adapter Evolution and Lifecycle
224	
225	4.1 Fitness-Driven Selection
226	
227	Each adapter is scored using a weighted fitness function:
228	
229	φ(a) = 0.7 · pass\_rate(a) + 0.3 · generalization(a)
230	
231	where pass\_rate is measured on the training task and generalization is
232	measured on held-out tasks of the same type. The weight α=0.7 biases
233	toward specialization; it is an ablation target.
234	
235	4.2 Evolution Operations
236	
237	  --------------- ------------------------------------------------------------ ------------------------------------------------------------------------
238	  **Operation**   **Trigger**                                                  **Effect**
239	  Consolidate     ≥5 adapters for a task type, top-3 similarity \> threshold   TIES-merge top-3 into domain-level adapter
240	  Update          New adapter has higher fitness for same task type            Archive old adapter, register new one
241	  Forget          Adapter fitness \< 0.3                                       Soft delete (is\_archived = 1)
242	  Merge           Periodic sweep (evolution\_interval seconds)                 TIES/DARE merge top-3 per task type; new generation = max(parents) + 1
243	  --------------- ------------------------------------------------------------ ------------------------------------------------------------------------
244	
245	4.3 TIES vs DARE Merging
246	
247	**TIES** (density=0.5) prunes 50% of parameters and resolves sign
248	conflicts by majority vote---preferred when adapters are diverse
249	(consolidating across subtask types). **DARE** (drop\_rate=0.1) randomly
250	drops 10% and rescales---preferred when adapters are similar (averaging
251	multiple runs of the same task). Both are implemented in merging.py.
252	
253	4.4 Lineage Tracking
254	
255	The adapter registry stores parent\_ids (JSON list) and generation
256	(integer) for every adapter, enabling full lineage tracing. Write-once
257	enforcement ensures adapter weights and hashes are immutable---only
258	metadata (fitness, archived status) is mutable.
259	
260	5\. Validation and Kill-Switch Gate
261	
262	5.1 Primary Gate: Pass\@1 Improvement
263	
264	The kill-switch requires ≥5% Pass\@1 improvement on HumanEval over the
265	unmodified base model:
266	
267	-   **Benchmark:** HumanEval, 20--30 held-out tasks, k=5 samples per
268	    task
269	
270	-   **Formula:** Pass\@1 = (1/N) ∑ 1\[task\_i passes on ≥1 of k
271	    samples\]
272	
273	-   **Expected range:** 5--15% improvement signals real effect
274	
275	-   **If gate fails:** Stop and reassess before investing further GPU
276	    time
277	
278	5.2 Baselines
279	
280	  ------------------------------- ------------------------------------------ ---------------------------------
281	  **Condition**                   **Description**                            **Purpose**
282	  Vanilla model                   Qwen3.5-9B base (bfloat16, no adapter)     Lower bound
283	  RAG baseline                    Retrieved trajectory snippets in context   Context-space memory ceiling
284	  QLoRA (DeltaCoder warm-start)   Stage 1 adapter on same trajectory data    Weight-space upper bound
285	  Hypernetwork-generated          Stage 2 single forward pass                Production system quality check
286	  ------------------------------- ------------------------------------------ ---------------------------------
287	
288	5.3 Diagnostic Metrics (Non-Gating)
289	
290	-   **Adapter Frobenius norm \|\|**ΔW**\|\|\_F:** Must be meaningfully
291	    nonzero---confirms adapter modifies model behavior
292	
293	-   **Inter-adapter cosine diversity \> 0.1:** Confirms diversity, not
294	    mode collapse
295	
296	-   **Training loss convergence:** Should decrease without early plateau
297	
298	5.4 PBB-Inspired Procedural Encoding Test
299	
300	Beyond Pass\@1: generate an adapter from a trajectory containing a
301	specific algorithm, load it, and query with held-out inputs *not in the
302	trajectory and not in context*. If the adapter-loaded model executes the
303	procedure better than the base model on novel inputs, the adapter has
304	encoded procedural knowledge in weight space.
305	
306	6\. Inference Configuration
307	
308	6.1 Temperature Recalibration
309	
310	The current optimized temperature of 0.25 was derived on
311	Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct. DeltaCoder's model card documents 0.5 as the
312	hard minimum for multi-turn agentic use. After migration, re-run
313	scripts/optimization/run\_optimization.py (Bayesian optimization, 200+
314	trials) on the new stack.
315	
316	6.2 Sampling Defaults
317	
318	  -------------------- ----------------- ----------- ----------- --------------------------------------
319	  **Context**          **Temperature**   **Top-k**   **Top-p**   **Notes**
320	  Multi-turn agentic   0.6               20          0.95        DeltaCoder model card recommendation
321	  Deterministic/code   0.2--0.4          ---         ---         Lower for consistent output
322	  Exploration phases   0.7--0.8          30          0.95        Higher for diverse decomposition
323	  Repetition penalty   1.04              ---         ---         Bayesian-optimized on previous stack
324	  -------------------- ----------------- ----------- ----------- --------------------------------------
325	
326	6.3 Serving Architecture
327	
328	vLLM with S-LoRA unified paging enables serving with up to 8 concurrent
329	adapters. Single-GPU training coordinates via sleep/wake endpoints.
330	Multi-GPU uses pipeline parallelism (not tensor parallelism---vLLM issue
331	\#21471 documents LoRA corruption on PCIe without NVLink).
332	
333	7\. Execution Plan
334	
335	7.1 Phase 0: Environment Validation (1--2 days)
336	
337	-   Load Qwen3.5-9B in vLLM with LoRA enabled; confirm GDN support
338	
339	-   Verify AWQ/GPTQ quantization availability
340	
341	-   Load DeltaCoder via PeftModel.from\_pretrained; confirm no shape
342	    errors
343	
344	-   Print state\_dict keys to confirm GDN module paths empirically
345	
346	-   Run HumanEval baseline (bare model, no adapter)
347

381	7.5 Timeline Summary

8	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

12	@dataclass
13	class GenerationResult:
14	    """Structured result returned by InferenceProvider.generate().
15	
16	    Attributes:
17	        text: The generated text output from the model.
18	        model: The model identifier used for generation.
19	        adapter_id: The LoRA adapter applied during generation, or None
20	            if no adapter was used.
21	        token_count: Total number of tokens consumed (prompt + completion).
22	        finish_reason: Reason generation stopped (e.g. "stop", "length").
23	
24	    Example:
25	        >>> result = GenerationResult(
26	        ...     text="def hello(): pass",
27	        ...     model="Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B",
28	        ...     adapter_id=None,
29	        ...     token_count=10,
30	        ...     finish_reason="stop",
31	        ... )
32	    """
33	
34	    text: str
35	    model: str
36	    adapter_id: str | None
37	    token_count: int
38	    finish_reason: str
39	
40	
41	class InferenceProvider(ABC):
42	    """Abstract base class for inference providers.
43	
44	    Defines a provider-agnostic API for text generation and LoRA adapter
45	    lifecycle management. All methods are async because every provider
46	    communicates over HTTP.
47	
48	    Concrete implementations:
49	        - VLLMProvider: Full LoRA support via vLLM's dynamic loading API.
50	        - OllamaProvider: Base-model inference only; adapter ops raise
51	          UnsupportedOperationError.
52	    """
53	
54	    @abstractmethod
55	    async def generate(
56	        self,
57	        prompt: str,
58	        model: str,
59	        adapter_id: str | None = None,
60	        max_tokens: int = 4096,
61	        system_prompt: str | None = None,
62	        temperature: float | None = None,
63	        top_p: float | None = None,
64	        repetition_penalty: float | None = None,
65	    ) -> GenerationResult:
66	        """Generate text from a prompt.
67	
68	        Args:
69	            prompt: The user-facing input prompt.
70	            model: The model identifier to use for generation.
71	            adapter_id: Optional LoRA adapter to apply during generation.
72	                If None, uses the base model directly.
73	            max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate.
74	            system_prompt: Optional system-level instruction. Providers that
75	                support chat templates will format this as a system message.
76	
77	            temperature: Sampling temperature override.
78	            top_p: Nucleus sampling threshold override.
79	            repetition_penalty: Repetition penalty override.
80	
81	        Returns:
82	            A GenerationResult containing the generated text and metadata.
83	
84	        Example:
85	            >>> result = await provider.generate("def hello", model="Qwen2.5-Coder-7B")
86	            >>> print(result.text)
87	        """
88	        ...
89	
90	    @abstractmethod
91	    async def load_adapter(self, adapter_id: str, adapter_path: str) -> None:
92	        """Load a LoRA adapter into the inference server.
93	
94	        Args:
95	            adapter_id: Unique name for the adapter (used as lora_name in vLLM).
96	            adapter_path: Filesystem path to the adapter weights directory.
97	
98	        Raises:
99	            UnsupportedOperationError: If the provider does not support adapters.
100	            httpx.HTTPStatusError: If the server returns an error response.
101	
102	        Example:
103	            >>> await provider.load_adapter("adapter-001", "/models/adapter-001")
104	        """
105	        ...
106	
107	    @abstractmethod
108	    async def unload_adapter(self, adapter_id: str) -> None:
109	        """Unload a previously loaded LoRA adapter.
110	
111	        Args:
112	            adapter_id: The adapter name to remove from the server.
113	
114	        Raises:
115	            UnsupportedOperationError: If the provider does not support adapters.
116	            httpx.HTTPStatusError: If the server returns an error response.
117	
118	        Example:
119	            >>> await provider.unload_adapter("adapter-001")
120	        """
121	        ...
122	
123	    @abstractmethod
124	    async def list_adapters(self) -> list[str]:
125	        """List all currently loaded LoRA adapters.
126	
127	        Returns:
128	            Sorted list of adapter IDs currently available for inference.
129	            Returns an empty list if no adapters are loaded or the provider
130	            does not support adapters.
131	
132	        Example:
133	            >>> adapters = await provider.list_adapters()
134	            >>> print(adapters)  # ["adapter-001", "adapter-002"]
135	        """
136	        ...

{"parentUuid":"895909a4-b29a-4299-ba99-f95d553c3d97","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"2cec357f-e1e0-45e7-b68c-d7320c59fa38","agentId":"ad40e130c6ec87fe3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LYZRkjjJuzzaBNjy4VRLTV","type":"tool_result","content":"1	"""AdapterRegistry class providing CRUD operations for adapter metadata."""
2	
3	import json
4	from typing import Any
5	
6	from shared.storage_utils import set_wal_mode
7	from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine
8	from sqlmodel import Session, SQLModel, col, select
9	
10	from adapter_registry.exceptions import AdapterAlreadyExistsError, AdapterNotFoundError
11	from adapter_registry.models import AdapterRecord
12	
13	
14	class AdapterRegistry:
15	    """Registry for storing and querying LoRA adapter metadata.
16	
17	    Provides CRUD operations backed by SQLite via SQLModel. The registry
18	    is initialized with a SQLAlchemy Engine and creates tables idempotently
19	    on construction. WAL mode is activated automatically on every new
20	    SQLite connection via an event hook registered before table creation.
21	
22	    Raises:
23	        AdapterAlreadyExistsError: When storing a duplicate adapter ID.
24	        AdapterNotFoundError: When querying for a non-existent adapter.
25	
26	    Example:
27	        >>> from sqlalchemy import create_engine
28	        >>> engine = create_engine("sqlite:///adapters.db")
29	        >>> registry = AdapterRegistry(engine=engine)
30	        >>> registry.store(record)
31	    """
32	
33	    def __init__(self, engine: Engine) -> None:
34	        """Initialize the registry with a SQLAlchemy Engine.
35	
36	        Registers a WAL-mode hook on the engine before creating tables to
37	        ensure every connection (including those opened by create_all) uses
38	        Write-Ahead Logging. Table creation is idempotent — safe to call
39	        against an existing database.
40	
41	        Args:
42	            engine: A SQLAlchemy Engine connected to the target SQLite database.
43	        """
44	        self._engine = engine
45	        set_wal_mode(engine)
46	        SQLModel.metadata.create_all(engine)
47	
48	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
49	    # Internal helpers
50	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
51	
52	    def _execute_query(self, stmt: Any) -> list[AdapterRecord]:
53	        """Execute a select statement and return detached AdapterRecord instances.
54	
55	        Centralises the ``with Session ... expunge`` boilerplate used by every
56	        read method so it is not repeated across the class.
57	
58	        Args:
59	            stmt: A SQLModel select statement returning AdapterRecord rows.
60	
61	        Returns:
62	            List of AdapterRecord instances detached from the session.
63	        """
64	        with Session(self._engine, expire_on_commit=False) as session:
65	            results = list(session.exec(stmt).all())
66	            for r in results:
67	                session.expunge(r)
68	            return results
69	
70	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
71	    # Write operations
72	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
73	
74	    def store(self, record: AdapterRecord) -> None:
75	        """Store a new adapter record in the registry.
76	
77	        Args:
78	            record: The AdapterRecord to persist.
79	
80	        Raises:
81	            AdapterAlreadyExistsError: If an adapter with the same ID exists.
82	
83	        Example:
84	            >>> engine = create_engine("sqlite:///adapters.db")
85	            >>> registry = AdapterRegistry(engine=engine)
86	            >>> record = AdapterRecord(id="abc-1", version=1, task_type="bug-fix",
87	            ...     base_model_id="qwen2.5-coder-7b", rank=16,
88	            ...     created_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00Z",
89	            ...     file_path="/adapters/abc-1.safetensors",
90	            ...     file_hash="sha256hash", file_size_bytes=4096,
91	            ...     source="distillation", session_id="sess-001")
92	            >>> registry.store(record)
93	        """
94	        with Session(self._engine, expire_on_commit=False) as session:
95	            if session.get(AdapterRecord, record.id) is not None:
96	                raise AdapterAlreadyExistsError(
97	                    f"Adapter '{record.id}' already exists."
98	                )
99	            session.add(record)
100	            session.commit()
101	            session.expunge(record)
102	
103	    def archive(self, adapter_id: str) -> None:
104	        """Archive an adapter by setting is_archived=True.
105	
106	        Args:
107	            adapter_id: ID of the adapter to archive.
108	
109	        Raises:
110	            AdapterNotFoundError: If no adapter with the given ID exists.
111	        """
112	        with Session(self._engine, expire_on_commit=False) as session:
113	            record = session.get(AdapterRecord, adapter_id)
114	            if record is None:
115	                raise AdapterNotFoundError(f"No adapter with id '{adapter_id}'.")
116	            record.is_archived = True
117	            session.add(record)
118	            session.commit()
119	
120	    def update_fitness(
121	        self,
122	        adapter_id: str,
123	        pass_rate: float,
124	        fitness_score: float,
125	    ) -> None:
126	        """Update evaluation metrics for an adapter.
127	
128	        Args:
129	            adapter_id: ID of the adapter to update.
130	            pass_rate: New pass rate value.
131	            fitness_score: New fitness score value.
132	
133	        Raises:
134	            AdapterNotFoundError: If no adapter with the given ID exists.
135	        """
136	        with Session(self._engine, expire_on_commit=False) as session:
137	            record = session.get(AdapterRecord, adapter_id)
138	            if record is None:
139	                raise AdapterNotFoundError(f"No adapter with id '{adapter_id}'.")
140	            record.pass_rate = pass_rate
141	            record.fitness_score = fitness_score
142	            session.add(record)
143	            session.commit()
144	
145	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
146	    # Read operations
147	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
148	
149	    def retrieve_by_id(self, adapter_id: str) -> AdapterRecord:
150	        """Retrieve a single adapter record by its unique ID.
151	
152	        Args:
153	            adapter_id: The unique identifier of the adapter to retrieve.
154	
155	        Returns:
156	            The matching AdapterRecord.
157	
158	        Raises:
159	            AdapterNotFoundError: If no adapter with the given ID exists.
160	
161	        Example:
162	            >>> engine = create_engine("sqlite:///adapters.db")
163	            >>> registry = AdapterRegistry(engine=engine)
164	            >>> record = registry.retrieve_by_id("abc-1")
165	        """
166	        with Session(self._engine, expire_on_commit=False) as session:
167	            record = session.get(AdapterRecord, adapter_id)
168	            if record is None:
169	                raise AdapterNotFoundError(f"No adapter with id '{adapter_id}'.")
170	            session.expunge(record)
171	            return record
172	
173	    def query_by_task_type(self, task_type: str) -> list[AdapterRecord]:
174	        """Query all adapters matching a given task type.
175	
176	        Args:
177	            task_type: The task category to filter by (e.g. 'bug-fix').
178	
179	        Returns:
180	            List of matching AdapterRecord instances. Empty list if none match.
181	
182	        Example:
183	            >>> engine = create_engine("sqlite:///adapters.db")
184	            >>> registry = AdapterRegistry(engine=engine)
185	            >>> results = registry.query_by_task_type("bug-fix")
186	        """
187	        stmt = select(AdapterRecord).where(AdapterRecord.task_type == task_type)
188	        return self._execute_query(stmt)
189	
190	    def list_all(self) -> list[AdapterRecord]:
191	        """List all non-archived adapter records.
192	
193	        Returns:
194	            List of all AdapterRecord instances where is_archived=False.
195	
196	        Example:
197	            >>> engine = create_engine("sqlite:///adapters.db")
198	            >>> registry = AdapterRegistry(engine=engine)
199	            >>> all_adapters = registry.list_all()
200	        """
201	        stmt = select(AdapterRecord).where(
202	            AdapterRecord.is_archived == False  # noqa: E712
203	        )
204	        return self._execute_query(stmt)
205	
206	    def query_best_for_task(
207	        self, task_type: str, top_k: int = 3
208	    ) -> list[AdapterRecord]:
209	        """Return the top-k highest-fitness adapters for a task type.
210	
211	        Args:
212	            task_type: Task category to filter by.
213	            top_k: Maximum number of results to return.
214	
215	        Returns:
216	            List of AdapterRecord instances ordered by fitness_score DESC.
217	        """
218	        stmt = (
219	            select(AdapterRecord)
220	            .where(
221	                AdapterRecord.task_type == task_type,
222	                AdapterRecord.is_archived == False,  # noqa: E712
223	                col(AdapterRecord.fitness_score).isnot(None),
224	            )
225	            .order_by(col(AdapterRecord.fitness_score).desc())
226	            .limit(top_k)
227	        )
228	        return self._execute_query(stmt)
229	
230	    def is_task_solved(self, task_hash: str, threshold: float = 0.95) -> bool:
231	        """Check if any adapter for the given task hash meets the threshold.
232	
233	        Args:
234	            task_hash: Training task hash to check.
235	            threshold: Minimum pass_rate to consider "solved".
236	
237	        Returns:
238	            True if a qualifying adapter exists.
239	        """
240	        stmt = (
241	            select(AdapterRecord)
242	            .where(
243	                AdapterRecord.training_task_hash == task_hash,
244	                AdapterRecord.is_archived == False,  # noqa: E712
245	                col(AdapterRecord.pass_rate) >= threshold,
246	            )
247	            .limit(1)
248	        )
249	        return bool(self._execute_query(stmt))
250	
251	    def get_lineage(self, adapter_id: str) -> list[str]:
252	        """Walk the parent_ids chain for an adapter.
253	
254	        Args:
255	            adapter_id: Starting adapter ID.
256	
257	        Returns:
258	            List of adapter IDs from the given adapter back to the root.
259	        """
260	        chain: list[str] = [adapter_id]
261	        current_id = adapter_id
262	        visited: set[str] = {adapter_id}
263	
264	        while True:
265	            with Session(self._engine, expire_on_commit=False) as session:
266	                record = session.get(AdapterRecord, current_id)
267	                if record is None or record.parent_ids is None:
268	                    break
269	                parent_list: list[str] = json.loads(record.parent_ids)
270	                if not parent_list:
271	                    break
272	                next_id = parent_list[0]
273	                if next_id in visited:
274	                    break
275	                visited.add(next_id)
276	                chain.append(next_id)
277	                current_id = next_id
278	
279	        return chain
280	
281	    def get_lineage_dag(self, adapter_id: str) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
282	        """Return the full multi-parent lineage DAG as an adjacency dict.
283	
284	        Unlike ``get_lineage`` which follows only the first parent, this
285	        method does BFS over all parents to capture the complete merge tree.
286	
287	        Args:
288	            adapter_id: Starting adapter ID.
289	
290	        Returns:
291	            Dict mapping each ancestor adapter ID to its list of parent IDs.
292	            Unknown IDs encountered during traversal are recorded with an
293	            empty parent list (no exception raised).
294	
295	        Note:
296	            Missing adapter IDs are silently treated as leaf nodes.
297	        """
298	        from collections import deque
299	
300	        adjacency: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
301	        queue: deque[str] = deque([adapter_id])
302	        visited: set[str] = {adapter_id}
303	
304	        while queue:
305	            current_id = queue.popleft()
306	            with Session(self._engine, expire_on_commit=False) as session:
307	                record = session.get(AdapterRecord, current_id)
308	                if record is None or record.parent_ids is None:
309	                    adjacency[current_id] = []
310	                    continue
311	                parent_list: list[str] = json.loads(record.parent_ids)
312	                adjacency[current_id] = parent_list
313	                for pid in parent_list:
314	                    if pid not in visited:
315	                        visited.add(pid)
316	                        queue.append(pid)
317	
318	        return adjacency
319	
320	    def query_unevaluated(self, task_type: str | None = None) -> list[AdapterRecord]:
321	        """Return adapters that have not been evaluated (pass_rate IS NULL).
322	
323	        Args:
324	            task_type: Optional task type filter.
325	
326	        Returns:
327	            List of unevaluated AdapterRecord instances.
328	        """
329	        stmt = select(AdapterRecord).where(
330	            AdapterRecord.is_archived == False,  # noqa: E712
331	            col(AdapterRecord.pass_rate).is_(None),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
332	        )
333	        if task_type is not None:
334	            stmt = stmt.where(AdapterRecord.task_type == task_type)
335	        return self._execute_query(stmt)
336	
337	    def get_task_types(self) -> list[str]:
338	        """Return all distinct task types in the registry.
339	
340	        Returns:
341	            Sorted list of unique task_type values.
342	        """
343	        with Session(self._engine, expire_on_commit=False) as session:
344	            stmt = select(AdapterRecord.task_type).distinct()
345	            results = list(session.exec(stmt).all())
346	            return sorted(results)

3	Provides SubprocessBackend (default) and NsjailBackend (Linux-only) for

21	@dataclass(frozen=True)
22	class SandboxResult:
23	    """Result of executing code in a sandbox.
24	
25	    Attributes:
26	        stdout: Captured standard output.
27	        stderr: Captured standard error.
28	        exit_code: Process exit code (0 = success).
29	        is_timed_out: Whether execution was terminated due to timeout.
30	    """
31	
32	    stdout: str
33	    stderr: str
34	    exit_code: int
35	    is_timed_out: bool

38	class SandboxBackend(ABC):
39	    """Abstract base class for sandbox execution backends."""
40	
41	    @abstractmethod
42	    def run(self, code: str, timeout: int = 30) -> SandboxResult:
43	        """Execute Python code and return the result.
44	
45	        Args:
46	            code: Python source code to execute.
47	            timeout: Maximum execution time in seconds.
48	
49	        Returns:
50	            A SandboxResult with captured output and exit status.
51	        """
52	
53	
54	class SubprocessBackend(SandboxBackend):
55	    """Execute code via a standard subprocess.
56	
57	    Writes code to a temporary file and runs it with the current Python
58	    interpreter. This is the default backend, used on all platforms.
59	    """
60	
61	    def run(self, code: str, timeout: int = 30) -> SandboxResult:
62	        """Execute Python code in a subprocess.
63	
64	        Args:
65	            code: Python source code to execute.
66	            timeout: Maximum execution time in seconds.
67	
68	        Returns:
69	            A SandboxResult with captured output and exit status.
70	        """
71	        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
72	            script_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "solution.py")
73	            with open(script_path, "w") as f:
74	                f.write(code)
75	
76	            try:
77	                proc = subprocess.run(
78	                    [sys.executable, script_path],
79	                    capture_output=True,
80	                    text=True,
81	                    timeout=timeout,
82	                    cwd=tmpdir,
83	                )
84	                return SandboxResult(
85	                    stdout=proc.stdout,
86	                    stderr=proc.stderr,
87	                    exit_code=proc.returncode,
88	                    is_timed_out=False,
89	                )
90	            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
91	                return SandboxResult(
92	                    stdout="",
93	                    stderr=f"Execution timed out after {timeout}s",
94	                    exit_code=1,
95	                    is_timed_out=True,
96	                )
97

99	class NsjailBackend(SandboxBackend):
100	    """Execute code inside an nsjail sandbox (Linux-only).
101	
102	    Falls back to SubprocessBackend if nsjail is not found on PATH.
103	    Applies resource limits via nsjail flags: --time_limit, --rlimit_as,
104	    --rlimit_cpu.
105	    """
106	
107	    def __init__(self) -> None:
108	        """Initialize NsjailBackend, checking nsjail availability."""
109	        self._nsjail_path = shutil.which("nsjail")
110	        self._fallback = SubprocessBackend()
111	        if self._nsjail_path is None:
112	            logger.warning(
113	                "nsjail not found on PATH; NsjailBackend will fall back to subprocess"
114	            )
115	
116	    def run(self, code: str, timeout: int = 30) -> SandboxResult:
117	        """Execute Python code inside nsjail, or fall back to subprocess.
118	
119	        Args:
120	            code: Python source code to execute.
121	            timeout: Maximum execution time in seconds.
122	
123	        Returns:
124	            A SandboxResult with captured output and exit status.
125	        """
126	        if self._nsjail_path is None:
127	            return self._fallback.run(code, timeout)
128	
129	        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
130	            script_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "solution.py")
131	            with open(script_path, "w") as f:
132	                f.write(code)
133	
134	            cmd = [
135	                self._nsjail_path,
136	                "--mode",
137	                "o",
138	                "--time_limit",
139	                str(timeout),
140	                "--rlimit_as",
141	                "512",
142	                "--rlimit_cpu",
143	                str(timeout),
144	                "--",
145	                sys.executable,
146	                script_path,
147	            ]
148	
149	            try:
150	                proc = subprocess.run(
151	                    cmd,
152	                    capture_output=True,
153	                    text=True,
154	                    timeout=timeout + 5,
155	                    cwd=tmpdir,
156	                )
157	                return SandboxResult(
158	                    stdout=proc.stdout,
159	                    stderr=proc.stderr,
160	                    exit_code=proc.returncode,
161	                    is_timed_out=False,
162	                )
163	            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
164	                return SandboxResult(
165	                    stdout="",
166	                    stderr=f"Execution timed out after {timeout}s",
167	                    exit_code=1,
168	                    is_timed_out=True,
169	                )

172	def count_test_results(stdout: str, stderr: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
173	    """Parse unittest output to count (passed, total) tests.
174	
175	    Args:
176	        stdout (str): Captured standard output from test execution.
177	        stderr (str): Captured standard error from test execution.
178	
179	    Returns:
180	        tuple[int, int]: (passed_count, total_count). Returns (0, 0)
181	            if no unittest output is detected.
182	    """
183	    import re as _re
184	
185	    total = 0
186	    failed = 0
187	
188	    ran_match = _re.search(r"Ran (\d+) test", stderr or "")
189	    if ran_match:
190	        total = int(ran_match.group(1))
191	
192	    fail_match = _re.search(r"failures=(\d+)", stderr or "")
193	    err_match = _re.search(r"errors=(\d+)", stderr or "")
194	    if fail_match:
195	        failed += int(fail_match.group(1))
196	    if err_match:
197	        failed += int(err_match.group(1))
198	
199	    passed = max(0, total - failed)
200	    return passed, total
201	
202	
203	def extract_failed_tests(stderr: str) -> str:
204	    """Extract names of failing tests from unittest stderr.
205	
206	    Args:
207	        stderr (str): Captured standard error from test execution.
208	
209	    Returns:
210	        str: Semicolon-separated string of up to 5 failed test names.
211	            Empty string if no failures found.
212	    """
213	    if not stderr:
214	        return ""
215	    lines = stderr.strip().splitlines()
216	    failed = [
217	        ln.strip()
218	        for ln in lines
219	        if ln.strip().startswith("FAIL:") or ln.strip().startswith("ERROR:")
220	    ]
221	    return "; ".join(failed[:5])
222	
223	
224	def has_unittest_classes(code: str) -> bool:
225	    """Check whether code contains unittest.TestCase subclasses.
226	
227	    Args:
228	        code (str): Python source code string.
229	
230	    Returns:
231	        bool: True if the code contains at least one TestCase class
232	            definition.
233	    """
234	    import re as _re
235	
236	    return bool(_re.search(r"class\s+\w+\s*\(.*\bTestCase\b.*\)", code))
237	
238	
239	def get_sandbox_backend() -> SandboxBackend:
240	    """Return the appropriate sandbox backend based on environment.
241	
242	    Reads RUNE_EXEC_BACKEND env var:
243	    - "nsjail" → NsjailBackend (falls back to subprocess if nsjail not found)
244	    - anything else or unset → SubprocessBackend
245	
246	    Returns:
247	        A SandboxBackend instance.
248	    """
249	    backend = os.environ.get("RUNE_EXEC_BACKEND", "subprocess")
250	    if backend == "nsjail":
251	        return NsjailBackend()
252	    return SubprocessBackend()

4	
5	**Goal:** Ship a `model_training.reconstruction` subpackage that turns a directory tree of QLoRA oracle adapters (indexed by Rune's AdapterRegistry) into a T2L-compatible reconstruction-training manifest — the exact `{task_embs, lora_A, lora_B}` substrate that a future Sakana-style hypernetwork will learn to regress against.
6	
7	**Architecture:** Read-only reducer. Query AdapterRegistry → load each adapter's safetensors → extract per-(module, layer) A/B matrices → embed each task's description → emit a manifest + per-task embeddings + optional z-score stats. Adapter weights stay where they are; only index artifacts are written. Mirrors `text-to-lora/src/hyper_llm_modulator/data.py::get_recon_train_data` shape so a downstream hypernetwork can consume the manifest without further transformation.
8	
9	**Tech Stack:** Python 3.12, `safetensors`, `torch`, `sentence-transformers` (deferred), `sqlmodel` (via AdapterRegistry), `argparse`. GPU imports stay inside function bodies per INFRA-05.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Source of Truth for the Target Shape
14	
15	From T2L's `get_recon_train_data` (verified via WebFetch 2026-04-22), each oracle record must supply:
16	
17	- `layer_indices: {module_name: LongTensor}` — sorted layer indices per module
18	- `lora_A: {module_name: Tensor[n_layers, rank, in_features]}`
19	- `lora_B: {module_name: Tensor[n_layers, out_features, rank]}`
20	
21	PEFT stores A as `(rank, in_features)` and B as `(out_features, rank)` per layer, so stacking along dim 0 yields the T2L shapes **without** any transposes. Do not transpose B.
22	
23	Task embedding: `torch.Tensor[1, hidden_size]` per task, keyed by task_id. For a real embedding model we use `sentence-transformers/all-mpnet-base-v2` (768-d) unless overridden; we also support a `None` embedding model, in which case we emit orthogonal one-hot vectors (matching T2L's `torch.eye` fallback).
24	
25	## DeltaCoder-Relative Semantics (Critical)
26	
27	Per `2026-04-22-pr-28-training-upgrade-fit-assessment.yaml` line 157–160, `trainer.save_model()` writes only the adapter delta, not a base-merged adapter. That means the A/B matrices we extract are deltas **on top of DeltaCoder**, not on top of raw Qwen3.5-9B. The manifest MUST record the warm-start adapter so downstream consumers load the correct coordinate system at train/inference time. See `manifest.ReconstructionManifest.warm_start_adapter`.

21	def calculate_pass_at_k(n_samples: int, n_correct: int, k: int = 1) -> float:
22	    """Calculate the Pass@k metric for code generation evaluation.
23	
24	    Computes the probability that at least one of k sampled solutions is
25	    correct, using the unbiased estimator from the HumanEval paper
26	    (Chen et al., 2021). This avoids the high-variance naive estimator.
27	
28	    Formula: 1 - prod((n-c-i)/(n-i) for i in range(k))
29	
30	    Args:
31	        n_samples: Total number of samples generated per problem.
32	        n_correct: Number of correct samples out of n_samples.
33	        k: Number of attempts allowed for the Pass@k metric. Defaults to 1.
34	
35	    Returns:
36	        Pass@k probability as a float between 0.0 and 1.0.
37	
38	    Raises:
39	        ValueError: If n_correct > n_samples.
40	
41	    Example:
42	        >>> score = calculate_pass_at_k(n_samples=100, n_correct=85, k=1)
43	        >>> score
44	        0.85
45	    """
46	    if n_correct > n_samples:
47	        raise ValueError(
48	            f"n_correct ({n_correct}) cannot be greater than n_samples ({n_samples})"
49	        )
50	    if n_correct == n_samples:
51	        return 1.0
52	    if n_samples - n_correct < k:
53	        return 1.0
54	    # Unbiased estimator: 1 - prod((n-c-i)/(n-i) for i in range(k))
55	    product = math.prod((n_samples - n_correct - i) / (n_samples - i) for i in range(k))
56	    return 1.0 - product
57

59	def run_kill_switch_gate(
60	    baseline_pass1: float,
61	    adapter_pass1: float,
62	    threshold: float = 0.05,
63	) -> dict[str, object]:
64	    """Compare baseline vs adapter Pass@1 scores and return a PASS/FAIL verdict.
65	
66	    Computes the relative improvement of the adapter over the baseline and
67	    returns PASS if the improvement meets the threshold, FAIL otherwise.
68	
69	    Args:
70	        baseline_pass1: Pass@1 score for the baseline model (no adapter).
71	        adapter_pass1: Pass@1 score for the adapter model.
72	        threshold: Minimum required relative improvement (default 0.05 = 5%).
73	
74	    Returns:
75	        Dictionary with:
76	            - "baseline_pass1": float
77	            - "adapter_pass1": float
78	            - "relative_delta": float, relative improvement over baseline
79	            - "verdict": str, "PASS" or "FAIL"
80	
81	    Example:
82	        >>> result = run_kill_switch_gate(0.50, 0.55)
83	        >>> result["verdict"]
84	        'PASS'
85	    """
86	    # 1e-9 prevents division-by-zero when baseline is 0.0
87	    relative_delta = (adapter_pass1 - baseline_pass1) / max(baseline_pass1, 1e-9)
88	    verdict = "PASS" if adapter_pass1 >= baseline_pass1 * (1 + threshold) else "FAIL"
89	
90	    print(
91	        f"Kill-switch gate result: {verdict}\n"
92	        f"  Baseline  Pass@1: {baseline_pass1:.4f}\n"
93	        f"  Adapter   Pass@1: {adapter_pass1:.4f}\n"
94	        f"  Relative delta:   {relative_delta:+.2%} (threshold: {threshold:+.0%})"
95	    )
96	
97	    return {
98	        "baseline_pass1": baseline_pass1,
99	        "adapter_pass1": adapter_pass1,
100	        "relative_delta": relative_delta,
101	        "verdict": verdict,
102	    }

105	def run_humaneval_subset(
106	    adapter_id: Optional[str],
107	    subset_size: int = 20,
108	    model: str = "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B",
109	    completions: Optional[dict[str, str]] = None,
110	) -> dict[str, Any]:
111	    """Run a HumanEval benchmark subset to evaluate an adapter.
112	
113	    Loads tasks from the bundled 20-task HumanEval subset JSON and evaluates
114	    them using the provided completions. For each task, concatenates
115	    prompt + completion + test + check(entry_point), writes to a temp script,
116	    and executes via subprocess. Exit code 0 = passed.
117	
118	    Args:
119	        adapter_id: UUID of the adapter to test (None = baseline, no adapter).
120	            Currently informational; inference wiring happens at a higher level.
121	        subset_size: Ignored — always uses the fixed 20-task bundled subset.
122	        model: Base model name (informational only, not used for inference here).
123	        completions: Dict mapping task_id -> completion string. Only tasks with
124	            an entry in this dict are evaluated. If None or empty, returns empty
125	            results.
126	
127	    Returns:
128	        Dictionary with benchmark results including:
129	            - "pass_count": int, number of tasks passed
130	            - "fail_count": int, number of tasks failed
131	            - "pass_rate": float, fraction of tasks passed (0.0 to 1.0)
132	            - "task_results": list of per-task result dicts with task_id, passed
133	            - "summary": str, human-readable summary
134	
135	    Raises:
136	        NotImplementedError: If completions are not provided (inference not wired).
137	
138	    Example:
139	        >>> completions = {"HumanEval/0": "    return []"}
140	        >>> results = run_humaneval_subset(adapter_id=None, completions=completions)
141	        >>> results["pass_count"]
142	        0
143	    """
144	    if completions is None:
145	        raise NotImplementedError("run_humaneval_subset is not yet implemented.")
146	
147	    # Load bundled task data
148	    subset_path = _DATA_DIR / "humaneval_subset.json"
149	    with subset_path.open() as f:
150	        all_tasks: list[dict[str, str]] = json.load(f)
151	
152	    # Build lookup by task_id
153	    task_map = {t["task_id"]: t for t in all_tasks}
154	
155	    task_results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
156	
157	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
158	        for task_id, completion in completions.items():
159	            task = task_map.get(task_id)
160	            if task is None:
161	                continue
162	
163	            # Build executable script: prompt + completion + test + check(entry_point)
164	            script = (
165	                task["prompt"]
166	                + completion
167	                + "\n"
168	                + task["test"]
169	                + f"\ncheck({task['entry_point']})\n"
170	            )
171	
172	            script_path = Path(tmpdir) / f"{task_id.replace('/', '_')}.py"
173	            script_path.write_text(script)
174	
175	            passed = safe_subprocess_run(script_path, cwd=tmpdir)
176	            task_results.append({"task_id": task_id, "passed": passed})
177	
178	    pass_count = sum(1 for r in task_results if r["passed"])
179	    fail_count = len(task_results) - pass_count
180	    total = len(task_results)
181	    pass_rate = pass_count / total if total > 0 else 0.0
182	
183	    summary = (
184	        f"HumanEval subset: {pass_count}/{total} passed "
185	        f"(pass_rate={pass_rate:.2%}, adapter_id={adapter_id})"
186	    )
187	    print(summary)
188	
189	    return {
190	        "pass_count": pass_count,
191	        "fail_count": fail_count,
192	        "pass_rate": pass_rate,
193	        "task_results": task_results,
194	        "summary": summary,
195	    }

198	def score_adapter_quality(
199	    adapter_id: str,
200	    pass_rate: float,
201	    generalization_delta: float | None = None,
202	) -> float:
203	    """Compute an overall quality score for an adapter.
204	
205	    Aggregates the adapter's benchmark pass rate with its generalization
206	    performance (if available) into a single scalar quality score. When
207	    generalization data is absent, quality is derived from pass rate alone.
208	
209	    Args:
210	        adapter_id: UUID of the adapter to score.
211	        pass_rate: Fraction of benchmark tasks passed, in range 0.0 to 1.0.
212	        generalization_delta: Optional difference between in-distribution and
213	            out-of-distribution (OOD) performance. Positive values indicate
214	            the adapter generalizes well; negative values indicate overfitting.
215	            Defaults to None (not measured).
216	
217	    Returns:
218	        Quality score as a float between 0.0 and 1.0. Higher values indicate
219	        a better overall adapter quality.
220	
221	    Raises:
222	        NotImplementedError: score_adapter_quality is not yet implemented.
223	
224	    Example:
225	        >>> score = score_adapter_quality("adapter-001", pass_rate=0.85)
226	        >>> score
227	        0.85
228	        >>> score_with_gen = score_adapter_quality(
229	        ...     "adapter-001", 0.85, generalization_delta=0.1,
230	        ... )
231	        >>> score_with_gen > score
232	        True
233	    """
234	    if generalization_delta is not None:
235	        quality = min(pass_rate + 0.1 * max(generalization_delta, 0), 1.0)
236	    else:
237	        quality = pass_rate
238	    return quality

269	        >>> results["best_adapter"] in ["adapter-001", "adapter-002"]
270	        True
271	        >>> results["best_adapter"] in results["rankings"]
272	        True
273	    """
274	    if len(adapter_ids) < 2:
275	        raise ValueError("compare_adapters requires at least 2 adapter IDs")
276	
277	    # Without live inference, return a stub comparison based on adapter order

{"parentUuid":"c8c8f685-5d0d-4fbf-8e47-3f538832b850","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"2cec357f-e1e0-45e7-b68c-d7320c59fa38","agentId":"ad40e130c6ec87fe3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0119BwsKiPZqQGPkC99a6Zra","type":"tool_result","content":"1	"""Out-of-distribution benchmark for adapter generalization testing.
2	
3	Provides functions for evaluating adapter performance on tasks outside
4	the training distribution, measuring generalization capability.
5	"""
6	
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import json
10	import tempfile
11	from pathlib import Path
12	from typing import Any
13	
14	from evaluation.utils import safe_subprocess_run
15	
16	_OOD_DATA_DIR = Path(__file__).parent / "data"
17	
18	
19	def run_ood_benchmark(
20	    adapter_id: str | None,
21	    completions: dict[str, str],
22	    benchmark_name: str = "ood_python",
23	) -> dict[str, Any]:
24	    """Run an out-of-distribution benchmark on provided completions.
25	
26	    Evaluates completions against OOD tasks from the bundled task set.
27	    Each task's prompt + completion is executed in a subprocess with its
28	    test harness.
29	
30	    Args:
31	        adapter_id: UUID of the adapter being tested (informational).
32	        completions: Dict mapping task_id to completion string.
33	        benchmark_name: Name of the OOD benchmark set.
34	
35	    Returns:
36	        Dictionary with ood_pass_rate and per-task results.
37	    """
38	    ood_path = _OOD_DATA_DIR / "ood_tasks.json"
39	    with ood_path.open() as f:
40	        all_tasks: list[dict[str, str]] = json.load(f)
41	
42	    task_map = {t["task_id"]: t for t in all_tasks}
43	    task_results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
44	
45	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
46	        for task_id, completion in completions.items():
47	            task = task_map.get(task_id)
48	            if task is None:
49	                continue
50	
51	            script = task["prompt"] + completion + "\n" + task["test"] + "\n"
52	            script_path = Path(tmpdir) / f"{task_id.replace('/', '_')}.py"
53	            script_path.write_text(script)
54	
55	            passed = safe_subprocess_run(script_path, cwd=tmpdir)
56	
57	            task_results.append({"task_id": task_id, "passed": passed})
58	
59	    total = len(task_results)
60	    pass_count = sum(1 for r in task_results if r["passed"])
61	    ood_pass_rate = pass_count / total if total > 0 else 0.0
62	
63	    return {
64	        "adapter_id": adapter_id,
65	        "benchmark_name": benchmark_name,
66	        "ood_pass_rate": ood_pass_rate,
67	        "pass_count": pass_count,
68	        "total": total,
69	        "task_results": task_results,
70	    }
71	
72	
73	def compute_generalization_delta(
74	    in_dist_rate: float,
75	    ood_rate: float,
76	) -> float:
77	    """Compute the generalization delta between in-distribution and OOD performance.
78	
79	    A positive delta means the adapter does better on OOD tasks relative to
80	    in-distribution performance. A negative delta indicates overfitting.
81	
82	    Args:
83	        in_dist_rate: Pass rate on in-distribution tasks (0.0 to 1.0).
84	        ood_rate: Pass rate on OOD tasks (0.0 to 1.0).
85	
86	    Returns:
87	        Generalization delta as ood_rate - in_dist_rate.
88	    """
89	    return ood_rate - in_dist_rate
90	"}]},"uuid":"81fa1015-0367-4165-9f68-77a60d574295","timestamp":"2026-04-22T13:11:24.703Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"c8c8f685-5d0d-4fbf-8e47-3f538832b850","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune","sessionId":"ae9e33d2-bead-4b95-a408-f0635bc64994","version":"2.1.116","gitBranch":"feat/training-upgrade","slug":"fluffy-drifting-salamander"}

198	def _resolve_warm_start(raw: str | None) -> str | None:
199	    """Map --warm-start alias/string to a concrete adapter id/path or None."""
200	    if raw is None:

202	    key = raw.strip().lower()
203	    if key in _WARM_START_ALIASES:
204	        return _WARM_START_ALIASES[key]
205	    return raw  # treat as explicit HF repo id or local path

209	    """Build the kwargs dict passed to train_and_register.

212	    without running the trainer itself.

241	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
242	    """Parse argv, dispatch to train_and_register (or dry-run and exit)."""
243	    parser = _build_parser()

29	def test_pass_at_k_perfect_score() -> None:
30	    """calculate_pass_at_k(1, 1, 1) returns 1.0."""

42	    assert abs(result - 0.85) < 1e-9

45	def test_pass_at_k_unbiased_estimator() -> None:
46	    """calculate_pass_at_k(10, 5, 3) returns correct unbiased estimator value.
47	
48	    Formula: 1 - prod((n-c-i)/(n-i) for i in range(k))
49	    n=10, c=5, k=3
50	    term i=0: (10-5-0)/(10-0) = 5/10
51	    term i=1: (10-5-1)/(10-1) = 4/9
52	    term i=2: (10-5-2)/(10-2) = 3/8
53	    prod = (5/10)*(4/9)*(3/8) = 60/720 = 1/12
54	    pass@3 = 1 - 1/12 = 11/12
55	    """
56	    expected = 1 - (5 / 10) * (4 / 9) * (3 / 8)
57	    result = calculate_pass_at_k(n_samples=10, n_correct=5, k=3)

1	meta:
2	  date: 2026-04-22
3	  title: "Phase-benchmark oracle pivot: replace per-(task,step) corpus with (5 phases x ~6 benchmarks) + self-distillation bootstrap"
4	  supersedes_sections_of:
5	    file: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-22-pr-28-training-upgrade-fit-assessment.yaml
6	    sections:
7	      - objective.terminal_goal      # refines episodic-memory claim to phase granularity
8	      - objective.training_mode_chosen
9	      - objective.rationale
10	      - deviations_from_t2l.keep.multi_step_trajectory_conditioning   # removed
11	      - gaps_blocking_terminal_goal.oracle_corpus_breadth             # rescoped
12	      - recommended_followups.priority_p0                             # reshuffled
13	  status: active_spec
14	  authored_by: noah+claude brainstorming session continuation
15	  adjacent_completed_work:
16	    - {ref: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-22-reconstruction-dataset-builder.md, status: landed}
17	    - {commit_range: "9f3ceec..8f64491", summary: "T2L reconstruction dataset builder subpackage; 42 tests; oracle-source-agnostic"}
18	
19	objective:
20	  terminal_goal: >
21	    Sakana-style Doc-to-LoRA hypernetwork that emits PHASE-SPECIALIZED LoRA adapters
22	    conditioned on (phase_id, task_description, trajectory_state_so_far), where phase
23	    is one of Rune's 5 pipeline roles (decompose, plan, code, integrate, diagnose).
24	    Each forward pass through the hypernetwork produces the adapter for the CURRENT
25	    phase given the TRAJECTORY ACCUMULATED SO FAR. This is "episodic memory in weight
26	    space" discretized at phase granularity rather than at arbitrary trajectory
27	    timestamps.
28	  training_mode_chosen: phase_benchmark_reconstruction_with_self_distillation
29	  rationale: >
30	    Reconstruction (L1 on oracle A/B matrices) is Sakana's validated path; we keep
31	    it. We replace "N tasks x K trajectory ticks" (unbounded corpus, fuzzy step
32	    semantics, no natively labeled training data) with "5 phases x ~6 coding
33	    benchmarks" (bounded 30-oracle corpus, crisp phase semantics, self-distillation
34	    data bootstrap via Pass@1 filtering). The phase axis preserves episodic memory
35	    as weight-space specialization per pipeline role; the benchmark axis gives
36	    native evaluability and direct Report_2 kill-switch wiring.
37	  non_goals:
38	    - tick_grained_trajectory_oracles    # dropped; phase-grained is sufficient
39	    - pure_sft_from_scratch              # reconstruction first per Sakana precedent
40	    - manual_phase_output_labeling       # self-distillation eliminates this
41	
42	phase_taxonomy:
43	  source_of_truth: libs/shared/src/shared/rune_models.py::PipelinePhase
44	  existing_values: [DECOMPOSE, PLAN, CODE, INTEGRATE]
45	  to_add: [DIAGNOSE]                      # currently implemented via templates + scripts but not enumerated
46	  per_phase_templates:
47	    decompose: libs/shared/src/shared/templates/decompose.j2
48	    plan:      libs/shared/src/shared/templates/plan.j2
49	    code:      libs/shared/src/shared/templates/code.j2
50	    integrate: libs/shared/src/shared/templates/integrate.j2
51	    diagnose:  libs/shared/src/shared/templates/diagnose.j2
52	  role_descriptions:
53	    decompose: "Break task into subtasks with [depends: N] declarations"
54	    plan:      "Per subtask, specify interfaces and approach"
55	    code:      "Implement subtask given predecessor interfaces (DAG-ordered)"
56	    integrate: "Combine subtask outputs into cohesive deliverable"
57	    diagnose:  "On failure, produce concise fix instruction for downstream repair"
58	
59	benchmark_selection:
60	  training_oracles:
61	    - {id: humaneval,      split: test,     n_problems: 164,  level: elementary}
62	    - {id: mbpp,           split: train,    n_problems: 374,  level: basic}
63	    - {id: apps,           split: train,    n_problems: 5000, level: intermediate}   # subsample for compute
64	    - {id: bigcodebench,   split: "v0.1.2", n_problems: 1140, level: applied}
65	    - {id: ds_1000,        split: all,      n_problems: 1000, level: data_science}
66	    - {id: livecodebench,  split: release_v4, n_problems: 500, level: competitive}
67	  heldout_for_generalization:
68	    - {id: swe_bench_lite,   n_problems: 300, rationale: "real-world multi-file"}
69	    - {id: codecontests,     n_problems: 200, rationale: "competitive, held out to test generalization from APPS/LCB"}
70	  exclusion_policy:
71	    - "No benchmark problems from held-out set may appear in oracle training data"
72	    - "APPS/LCB are training-time signals; codecontests evaluates generalization across competitive-style"
73	  total_oracles_target: 25     # 4 per-(phase,benchmark) phases x 6 benchmarks + 1 pooled diagnose (see q4 decision)
74	
75	corpus_bootstrap:
76	  method: self_distillation_filtered_by_pass_at_1
77	  inspiration: "STaR (Zelikman et al. 2022) - bootstrap reasoning traces via correctness filtering"
78	  procedure:
79	    - step: 1
80	      name: pipeline_run

meta:
  date: 2026-04-22
  title: "Phase-benchmark oracle pivot: replace per-(task,step) corpus with (5 phases x ~6 benchmarks) + self-distillation bootstrap"
  supersedes_sections_of:
    file: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-22-pr-28-training-upgrade-fit-assessment.yaml
    sections:
      - objective.terminal_goal      # refines episodic-memory claim to phase granularity
      - objective.training_mode_chosen
      - objective.rationale
      - deviations_from_t2l.keep.multi_step_trajectory_conditioning   # removed
      - gaps_blocking_terminal_goal.oracle_corpus_breadth             # rescoped
      - recommended_followups.priority_p0                             # reshuffled
  status: active_spec
  authored_by: noah+claude brainstorming session continuation
  adjacent_completed_work:
    - {ref: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-22-reconstruction-dataset-builder.md, status: landed}
    - {commit_range: "9f3ceec..8f64491", summary: "T2L reconstruction dataset builder subpackage; 42 tests; oracle-source-agnostic"}

objective:
  terminal_goal: >
    Sakana-style Doc-to-LoRA hypernetwork that emits PHASE-SPECIALIZED LoRA adapters
    conditioned on (phase_id, task_description, trajectory_state_so_far), where phase
    is one of Rune's 5 pipeline roles (decompose, plan, code, integrate, diagnose).
    Each forward pass through the hypernetwork produces the adapter for the CURRENT
    phase given the TRAJECTORY ACCUMULATED SO FAR. This is "episodic memory in weight
    space" discretized at phase granularity rather than at arbitrary trajectory
    timestamps.
  training_mode_chosen: phase_benchmark_reconstruction_with_self_distillation
  rationale: >
    Reconstruction (L1 on oracle A/B matrices) is Sakana's validated path; we keep
    it. We replace "N tasks x K trajectory ticks" (unbounded corpus, fuzzy step
    semantics, no natively labeled training data) with "5 phases x ~6 coding
    benchmarks" (bounded 30-oracle corpus, crisp phase semantics, self-distillation
    data bootstrap via Pass@1 filtering). The phase axis preserves episodic memory
    as weight-space specialization per pipeline role; the benchmark axis gives
    native evaluability and direct Report_2 kill-switch wiring.
  non_goals:
    - tick_grained_trajectory_oracles    # dropped; phase-grained is sufficient
    - pure_sft_from_scratch              # reconstruction first per Sakana precedent
    - manual_phase_output_labeling       # self-distillation eliminates this

phase_taxonomy:
  source_of_truth: libs/shared/src/shared/rune_models.py::PipelinePhase
  existing_values: [DECOMPOSE, PLAN, CODE, INTEGRATE]
  to_add: [DIAGNOSE]                      # currently implemented via templates + scripts but not enumerated
  per_phase_templates:
    decompose: libs/shared/src/shared/templates/decompose.j2
    plan:      libs/shared/src/shared/templates/plan.j2
    code:      libs/shared/src/shared/templates/code.j2
    integrate: libs/shared/src/shared/templates/integrate.j2
    diagnose:  libs/shared/src/shared/templates/diagnose.j2
  role_descriptions:
    decompose: "Break task into subtasks with [depends: N] declarations"
    plan:      "Per subtask, specify interfaces and approach"
    code:      "Implement subtask given predecessor interfaces (DAG-ordered)"
    integrate: "Combine subtask outputs into cohesive deliverable"
    diagnose:  "On failure, produce concise fix instruction for downstream repair"

benchmark_selection:
  training_oracles:
    - {id: humaneval,      split: test,     n_problems: 164,  level: elementary}
    - {id: mbpp,           split: train,    n_problems: 374,  level: basic}
    - {id: apps,           split: train,    n_problems: 5000, level: intermediate}   # subsample for compute
    - {id: bigcodebench,   split: "v0.1.2", n_problems: 1140, level: applied}
    - {id: ds_1000,        split: all,      n_problems: 1000, level: data_science}
    - {id: livecodebench,  split: release_v4, n_problems: 500, level: competitive}
  heldout_for_generalization:
    - {id: swe_bench_lite,   n_problems: 300, rationale: "real-world multi-file"}
    - {id: codecontests,     n_problems: 200, rationale: "competitive, held out to test generalization from APPS/LCB"}
  exclusion_policy:
    - "No benchmark problems from held-out set may appear in oracle training data"
    - "APPS/LCB are training-time signals; codecontests evaluates generalization across competitive-style"
  total_oracles_target: 25     # 4 per-(phase,benchmark) phases x 6 benchmarks + 1 pooled diagnose (see q4 decision)

corpus_bootstrap:
  method: self_distillation_filtered_by_pass_at_1
  inspiration: "STaR (Zelikman et al. 2022) - bootstrap reasoning traces via correctness filtering"
  procedure:
    - step: 1
      name: pipeline_run
